## [00:00 - 03:05]

First off, when I'm talking about bots, I always like to start with the misconception because I still see that this is this is true for most of the marketing community. So a lot of people think that bots or chat bots or messenger bots, whatever you want to call them, that bots are sort of like bobbleheads for your company. They're completely automated. They could answer anything that a customer might ask. If the customer asked the bot what its favorite color is, it would say blue. And the reason for this is because we have interacted with a lot of bots over the last 20 to 25 years online. But the bots of today, and the reason I picked this photo is because I think it's funny and it's a bobblehead. No support of Trump here. But most people think that this is what bots are in, that they're going to install a bot on their Facebook page and that it's going to be able to just start engaging with customers or prospects. At this stage in the game with bots, essentially what they are is a series of automation, really similar to CRNs that we use. And when we're building out different customer flows or different email sequences, this is what a bot is. This is inside of the tool mini chat. And you can see it's a series of messages that we're sending to people and based off of how they interact, we're then sending them other messages. And it sort of leads them down this continuum, down this path. It's the really cool part about Messenger is that you can have this super personal one-on-one conversation with these people. It's really unlike any other medium that we've had before because we can extract information from them that we never really have been able to before because we've never had this one-on-one conversation. And being able to extract that information, whether it's what they're interested in or maybe what their purchase intent is with your company, that then allows you to automate the customer experience so that the messages and the experience they're having with you is really specific to whatever information they gave you. So you can see this one, this was for a fitness client, a health client that I have. And you can see a question that we ask is what's your top health goal? Weight loss, detox or inflammation? And the reason we ask that and then tag them based off that interest is because later in the experience, I want to be able to follow up with them specifically about stuff that I know they're interested in. So keep that in mind. We're going to go through a ton of strategies today.

So really quickly, what are the rules for those of you guys that don't know? The really important rule to keep in mind when it comes to Messenger is the 24 plus one rule. And this basically means that you can only be directly promotional, which means asking someone to buy something or telling them about a sale, right? Or following up with them about an offer that you made. You can only be directly promotional to them within 24 hours of the last time they engaged with your bot, which again would be clicking a button or sending a message.
